message:
fix(google-docs): validate base_url + bind capability token to target resource URIs

Hardens the google-docs connector's capability check on every invoke so
that the presented token is verified *before* any business logic runs,
and verified against the specific document the caller is touching —
not just the declared operation capability. Also locks down the
test-only `base_url` override so a misconfigured or malicious caller
cannot aim the Docs client at an arbitrary HTTP host.

## base_url validation

New private helper `validate_docs_base_url(raw: &str)` is called from
`DocsConnector::configure` whenever `params["base_url"]` is present
and non-empty. It rejects the override as
`FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, ... }` when any of the
following hold:

- input is empty or whitespace-only;
- `Url::parse` fails;
- scheme is neither `http` nor `https`;
- host is missing;
- scheme is `http` but host is not a local-test host
  (`localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, `[::1]`);
- URL contains userinfo (`user:pass@…`);
- URL contains a query string or fragment;
- host is not a Google API host (exact match `googleapis.com` or a
  case-insensitive suffix match on `.googleapis.com`) and not a
  local-test host.

On success the helper trims trailing slashes and returns the
canonicalized string. The canonical form is then fed to
`DocsClient::with_base_url(...)`, so the client never sees an
un-sanitized value.

Supporting helpers:

- `is_local_test_host(host)` — keeps the local-test allowlist in one
  place, including the two spellings `::1` and `[::1]`.
- `host_is_googleapis(host)` — case-insensitive match on
  `googleapis.com` exact host or `.googleapis.com` suffix, so
  lookalikes like `googleapis.com.evil.com` and `evil-googleapis.com`
  are rejected.

## Token binding (verify_bound)

`DocsConnector::handle_invoke` is rearranged so the capability path
runs before the client call:

1. Parse `operation` into an `OperationId`
   (`InvalidRequest { code: 1002 }` on failure).
2. Introspect the connector's own operations table to look up the
   canonical capability string for `operation`; if the operation is
   not declared, fail with
   `FcpError::OperationNotGranted { operation }`. This means an
   unknown operation is now rejected *before* any capability-token
   parsing — matching the behavior other connectors exhibit and
   making `invoke_unknown_operation_is_denied_before_token_validation`
   a simpler, configure-free check.
3. Require `self.client` and `self.verifier` to be configured
   (`FcpError::NotConfigured` otherwise — previously only the client
   was required, so a half-configured connector could silently skip
   verification).
4. Read `params["capability_token"]` (missing →
   `InvalidRequest { code: 1001 }`; unparseable → same code with
   the serde error surfaced in the message).
5. Build `resource_uris_for_operation(operation, &input)` — a new
   helper that maps each operation to the canonical resource URI it
   should be bound to:

       docs.get           → ["google-docs:document:{document_id}"]
       docs.batch_update  → ["google-docs:document:{document_id}"]
       docs.create        → ["google-docs:documents"]

   Any operation outside this set gets `vec![]` (no resource
   binding), preserving the current behavior where host-routed ops
   don't assert a per-resource bind. Operations requiring a
   document id call `require_str(input, "document_id")` and surface
   `FcpError::InvalidRequest` on missing input.
6. Call `verifier.verify_bound(token, &cap_id, &op_id,
   &resource_uris)`. Previously this was
   `verifier.verify(token, &cap_id, &op_id, &[])`, which did not
   assert the token was scoped to the document in question.

## Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock

Adds `chrono.workspace = true` and `ciborium.workspace = true` to
`connectors/google-docs/Cargo.toml`'s `[dev-dependencies]` so the new
test-side token builder can produce real COSE capability tokens with
timestamped `validity(...)` and ciborium-serialized
`CapabilityConstraints`. `Cargo.lock` picks up the two additions under
the `google-docs` crate's dependency list; no other package versions
or transitive additions change.

## Tests

Inline `mod tests` grows a small harness:

- `build_test_token(signing_key, operation)` — mints a COSE token
  scoped to `docs.read` or `docs.write` as appropriate for the
  operation, with `resource_allow = ["*"]` constraints and the
  caller's `signing_key`, via
  `CapabilityTokenBuilder::new().try_constraints_cbor(...).sign(...)`.
- `configure_and_handshake(connector, signing_key)` — pair for
  wiring the connector through configure + handshake with the test
  verifying key before each invoke-success test.

New direct-unit tests for the new code:

- `validate_docs_base_url_accepts_googleapis` — canonical
  `https://docs.googleapis.com/v1/` trims to `.../v1`.
- `validate_docs_base_url_allows_localhost_http` —
  `http://localhost`, `http://127.0.0.1`, `http://[::1]:9999` all pass.
- `validate_docs_base_url_rejects_foreign_host` —
  `https://evil.example.com/...` fails with a message that mentions
  `googleapis.com`.
- `validate_docs_base_url_rejects_substring_smuggle` —
  `https://evil.com/docs.googleapis.com/...` fails (substring in
  path cannot smuggle past the host check).
- `validate_docs_base_url_rejects_query_fragment_userinfo` — three
  cases: `?leak=x`, `#frag`, and `attacker:pw@...` each fail, and
  the userinfo case's message mentions `userinfo`.
- `validate_docs_base_url_rejects_plain_http_on_public_host` —
  `http://docs.googleapis.com/v1` fails (plaintext only for local).
- `validate_docs_base_url_rejects_empty_and_malformed` — empty
  whitespace and unparseable URL both fail.
- `host_is_googleapis_rejects_lookalikes` — locks in the
  `googleapis.com.evil.com` / `evil-googleapis.com` negatives.
- `resource_uris_bind_docs_targets` — asserts `docs.get` with a
  `document_id` maps to `google-docs:document:{id}` and `docs.create`
  maps to `google-docs:documents`.

The existing `invoke_docs_get_missing_document_id`,
`invoke_docs_create_missing_title`,
`invoke_docs_batch_update_missing_requests`, and
`invoke_docs_batch_update_missing_document_id` tests now go through
`configure_and_handshake` and mint a real token via
`build_test_token(...)`, so they exercise the full token-verification
path rather than bypassing it.
